verses.text: God addresses 'his anointed,' Cyrus, whose hand He holds, to subdue nations and open gates; He calls him by name for Jacob's sake. Even a pagan king is His instrument. God names and uses whom He will to serve His people.

God addresses 'his anointed,' Cyrus, whose hand He holds, to subdue nations and open gates; He calls him by name for Jacob's sake. Even a pagan king is His instrument. God names and uses whom He will to serve His people.

God addresses 'his anointed,' Cyrus, whose hand He holds, to subdue nations and open gates; He calls him by name for Jacob's sake. Even a pagan king is His instrument. God names and uses whom He will to serve His people.

God addresses 'his anointed,' Cyrus, whose hand He holds, to subdue nations and open gates; He calls him by name for Jacob's sake. Even a pagan king is His instrument. God names and uses whom He will to serve His people.
'I am the LORD, and there is none else'; He forms light and darkness, makes peace and creates calamity. All is under His hand. The one God rules over good and ill alike, with no rival anywhere.
'I am the LORD, and there is none else'; He forms light and darkness, makes peace and creates calamity. All is under His hand. The one God rules over good and ill alike, with no rival anywhere.
'I am the LORD, and there is none else'; He forms light and darkness, makes peace and creates calamity. All is under His hand. The one God rules over good and ill alike, with no rival anywhere.

He bids the heavens drop righteousness, and warns, 'Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker'; shall the clay rebuke the potter? Man may not question God. The creature has no standing to dispute the wisdom of his Maker.

He bids the heavens drop righteousness, and warns, 'Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker'; shall the clay rebuke the potter? Man may not question God. The creature has no standing to dispute the wisdom of his Maker.

He bids the heavens drop righteousness, and warns, 'Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker'; shall the clay rebuke the potter? Man may not question God. The creature has no standing to dispute the wisdom of his Maker.

God who made the earth has 'raised him up in righteousness' to build His city and free His captives, not for price or reward. Cyrus serves freely God's plan. The LORD accomplishes His purposes through unwitting kings.

God who made the earth has 'raised him up in righteousness' to build His city and free His captives, not for price or reward. Cyrus serves freely God's plan. The LORD accomplishes His purposes through unwitting kings.

God who made the earth has 'raised him up in righteousness' to build His city and free His captives, not for price or reward. Cyrus serves freely God's plan. The LORD accomplishes His purposes through unwitting kings.

The wealth of nations shall come to God's people, who confess, 'Surely God is in thee'; Israel shall be 'saved... with an everlasting salvation.' Nations turn to Him. The salvation God gives His own is for ever, never to be ashamed.

God created the earth 'not in vain... to be inhabited,' and He speaks not in secret but in righteousness. His word is open and true. The God who made the world for life speaks plainly to save it.
God created the earth 'not in vain... to be inhabited,' and He speaks not in secret but in righteousness. His word is open and true. The God who made the world for life speaks plainly to save it.

There is no God beside Him, 'a just God and a Saviour'; so He calls, 'Look unto me, and be ye saved, all the ends of the earth.' Salvation is by looking to Him. The simplest turn of faith to God brings rescue to any soul.

God has sworn that 'unto me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear,' and in Him shall all be justified. Paul applies this to Christ. Every creature shall one day own the Lord, in whom alone is righteousness.
